evaluation_metrics.name: Incident Detection Lead Time
evaluation_metrics.description: The amount of time before a crowd incident occurs that the system detects and generates an alert.

baseline_from_papers.context: Traditional stadium operations are purely reactive, identifying crowd surges or bottlenecks only after congestion has already formed or physical alerts are raised.

verification_method.description: Run the Mesa crowd simulation framework or deterministic telemetry playback and measure the timestamp difference between the system-generated alert and the actual peak congestion state at Gate 7.

evaluation_metrics.name: Operator-to-Volunteer Dispatch Latency
evaluation_metrics.description: The latency from the moment the operator approves an action to when the role-aware multilingual task is delivered to the ground volunteer's mobile web device.

baseline_from_papers.context: Manual radio/dispatch coordination takes on average 8 minutes during peak event windows due to busy channels, translation delay, and verbal descriptions.

verification_method.description: Measure latency by comparing Firestore document timestamp of operator approval with the device receive confirmation logged in the database.

evaluation_metrics.name: Active Fan Rerouting Adoption Rate
evaluation_metrics.description: The percentage of fans en route to a congested zone who select an alternative recommended route after receiving a proactive nudge.

baseline_from_papers.context: Static stadium signs and standard navigation apps result in less than 15% of fans adopting secondary routes, as they default to visible queues.

verification_method.description: Run a test suite of 100 simulated fan agents en route to Gate 7, send the detour nudge, and verify that at least 40% change their coordinate pathing in Mesa.

evaluation_metrics.name: Context-Aware Instruction Accuracy
evaluation_metrics.description: Expert evaluation of LLM-generated translations of roles, situational scripts, and safety commands.

baseline_from_papers.context: Off-the-shelf automated machine translation engines fail to translate venue-specific jargon (e.g., 'Gate Marshal C7', 'soft perimeter') accurately in localized dialects.

verification_method.description: Cross-validate LLM-generated translations using a secondary LLM validator prompted with specialized dictionary constraints for stadium terminology.

evaluation_metrics.name: Accessibility Route Recalculation Time
evaluation_metrics.description: The time required to compute and push an alternative wheelchair-accessible route once an active path is flagged as blocked.

baseline_from_papers.context: Disabled fans wait an average of 10 minutes to resolve blockages because they must wait for staff interaction or search for an elevator manually.

verification_method.description: Inject a simulated blockage on an elevator route, trigger recalculation, and verify that a new valid ADA-compliant path is pushed to the client.

verification_protocol.step_1: Setup simulation environment with Mesa and preset scenarios.
verification_protocol.step_2: Run test script `npm run test:eval` which injects crowd sensors data to Firebase.
verification_protocol.step_3: Measure SentinelAI prediction confidence intervals using cross-validation over the generated data.
verification_protocol.step_4: Record dispatch times and fan routing decisions in Firebase metrics logs.
verification_protocol.step_5: Verify target metrics are achieved and compile performance report.
